expectations.text: A handoff markdown file was created in a thoughts/handoffs/ or thoughts/shared/handoffs/ directory
expectations.evidence: File created at thoughts/shared/handoffs/ via manual path construction

expectations.text: The handoff file has valid YAML frontmatter containing at least date and branch fields
expectations.evidence: Frontmatter has 12 fields including date, researcher, git_commit, branch, topic, tags, status, etc.

expectations.text: The handoff document is under 200 lines total
The Tasks section clearly separates the two concerns: code review feedback and cache invalidation feature
expectations.evidence: Lines 19-29: 'Task 1: Apply Code Review Feedback (PR #347)' and 'Task 2: Implement Cache Invalidation'

expectations.text: The Tasks section tracks which code review items are done (1-3) and which remain (4-5)
expectations.evidence: Lines 20-24: each item listed individually with ✅/❌ markers
Line 33: 'docs/code-review-feedback.md — Tracks all PR #347 feedback items'

expectations.text: The Learnings section mentions the write-through cache pattern discovery
expectations.evidence: Line 48: 'Cache Write-Through Pattern: The cache uses a write-through pattern where Set() is called on read-miss'

expectations.text: Action items include both remaining code review items (4-5) and cache invalidation wiring
expectations.evidence: Lines 65-73: five detailed action items covering both concerns

expectations.text: The document does not contain code blocks longer than 10 lines
The response to the user includes the /resume-handoff command with the handoff file path
expectations.evidence: Agent responded with '/resume-handoff' and file path

suggestions.reason: Old skill's 'Recent changes' section (lines 36-44) lists 7 line-level changes that are trivially discoverable via git diff. This is exactly the kind of content the new skill philosophy says to exclude.
'Artifacts' section (lines 56-61) largely repeats the Working Set — lists the same files already mentioned in Tasks and Recent changes.
Learnings items 2-4 (context threading, error wrapping, cache key consistency) describe standard Go patterns that are self-evident from reading the code, not non-obvious session discoveries.

eval_feedback.overall: Passes all assertions but 80 lines vs 48 for new skill. Contains git-discoverable content and explanations of self-evident patterns.
